content:
type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] in types.py (near top-level, after imports, before ImageBlock class). Behavior: given raw or base64-encoded image bytes, detect whether the input is already base64-encoded. Use base64.b64decode(image, validate=True) wrapped in try/except binascii.Error (import binascii): if decoding succeeds, the input was already base64-encoded -> encoded_image = image (unchanged), decoded_for_guessing = the decoded raw bytes. If it raises binascii.Error, the input was raw bytes -> encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image), decoded_for_guessing = image (the original raw bytes). Return (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing). Then in ImageBlock.image_to_base64 (the @model_validator(mode='after') method), replace the body's guessing+encoding logic with: if self.image: call self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); then if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None. Keep the early return self when not self.image. This must preserve existing behavior/tests: ImageBlock(image=<raw png bytes>) still ends up with self.image base64-encoded and correct mimetype guessed from the raw bytes.
note: normalize_image_bytes needs to compose with your get_image_mimetype - feel free to have get_image_mimetype accept decoded_for_guessing bytes if that fits your design better, just make sure image_to_base64 ends up calling filetype guessing on decoded (non-base64) bytes.
